techniqueGroups.name: Spaced Repetition
techniqueGroups.description: Reviewing material at increasing intervals strengthens long-term retention by leveraging the spacing effect.
techniqueGroups.practiceTip: This system uses SM-2 scheduling to optimize your review intervals based on mastery.

papers.title: Memory: A Contribution to Experimental Psychology
keyFindings: Discovered the forgetting curve — exponential decay of memory over time
Demonstrated that spaced repetition dramatically slows forgetting
Established the savings method for measuring memory retention

papers.title: Distributed practice in verbal recall tasks: A review and quantitative synthesis
keyFindings: Distributed practice significantly improves long-term retention
Optimal inter-study intervals depend on retention interval
Spacing effects are robust across diverse verbal recall tasks

papers.title: The right time to learn: Mechanisms and optimization of spaced learning
keyFindings: Spaced learning engages synaptic tagging and capture mechanisms
Optimal spacing intervals can be computationally modeled
Molecular mechanisms of spacing effect identified

papers.title: Spacing Repetitions Over Long Timescales: A Review and a Reconsolidation Explanation
keyFindings: Spacing effects persist over long timescales
Memory reconsolidation provides mechanistic explanation
Expanding schedules may optimize long-term retention

papers.title: Using spacing to enhance diverse forms of learning: Review and implications for instruction
keyFindings: Spacing enhances learning across diverse domains
Practical guidelines for implementing spacing in education
Spacing benefits extend beyond rote memorization

papers.title: A Meta-Analytic Review of the Benefit of Spacing out Retrieval Practice
keyFindings: Spacing retrieval practice produces robust learning benefits
Effect sizes moderate to large across studies
Benefits generalize across content domains

papers.title: Benefits of spaced learning are predicted by re-encoding of past experience in vmPFC
keyFindings: vmPFC re-encoding predicts spacing benefit magnitude
Neural signatures of spaced learning identified via fMRI
Individual differences in spacing benefit linked to brain activity

papers.title: Evidence of the Spacing Effect and Influences on Perceptions of Learning and Science Curricula
keyFindings: Spacing effect confirmed in science education contexts
Students underestimate spacing benefits due to metacognitive illusions
Curriculum design should incorporate spacing

papers.title: Spaced retrieval practice increases college students short- and long-term retention of mathematics knowledge
keyFindings: Spaced retrieval improves math retention in college students
Long-term benefits demonstrated for complex knowledge
Practical application in higher education confirmed

techniqueGroups.name: Active Recall & Testing Effect
techniqueGroups.description: Actively retrieving information from memory strengthens neural pathways far more effectively than passive review.
techniqueGroups.practiceTip: Flashcards and fill-in-the-blank modes leverage the testing effect — retrieval IS the learning event.

papers.title: The Power of Testing Memory: Basic Research and Implications for Educational Practice
keyFindings: Testing is a powerful learning event, not just assessment
Direct and indirect benefits of testing on memory
Practical implications for self-directed learning

papers.title: Test-enhanced learning: Taking memory tests improves long-term retention
keyFindings: Taking tests produces greater retention than restudying
Testing effect robust across multiple retention intervals
Free recall tests most effective for long-term memory

papers.title: Repeated retrieval during learning is the key to long-term retention
keyFindings: Repeated retrieval is more effective than repeated study
Dropping items from further study after retrieval does not impair retention
Retrieval practice is the key driver of durable memory

papers.title: Retrieval practice produces more learning than elaborative studying with concept mapping
keyFindings: Retrieval practice outperforms concept mapping for retention
Active retrieval more effective than elaborative encoding
Results challenge primacy of elaboration in learning

papers.title: Retrieval practice enhances new learning: The forward effect of testing
keyFindings: Prior retrieval practice enhances encoding of new material
Forward testing effect reduces proactive interference
Testing benefits extend beyond tested material

papers.title: Retrieval Practice in Classroom Settings: A Review of Applied Research
keyFindings: Retrieval practice effective in real classroom settings
Benefits transfer to applied educational contexts
Effect sizes moderate to large in field studies

papers.title: Investigating the testing effect: Retrieval as a characteristic of effective study strategies
keyFindings: Retrieval is the active ingredient in effective study strategies
Strategies that incorporate retrieval outperform those that do not
Testing effect confirmed across multiple experimental conditions

papers.title: Optimized schedules of retrieval practice for durable and efficient learning
keyFindings: Criterion-based retrieval schedules optimize learning efficiency
Three correct retrievals per item is near-optimal
Schedules can be individualized for maximum benefit

papers.title: Effects of retrieval practice on retention and application of complex educational concepts
keyFindings: Retrieval practice benefits extend to complex concepts
Application and transfer improved by retrieval practice
Effects persist over extended retention intervals

techniqueGroups.name: Evidence-Based Study Techniques
techniqueGroups.description: Research identifies which study strategies actually work — and which popular ones don't.
techniqueGroups.practiceTip: Practice testing and distributed practice are the two most effective techniques identified by research.

papers.title: Improving students learning with effective learning techniques
keyFindings: 10 learning techniques evaluated for effectiveness
Practice testing and distributed practice most effective
Comprehensive evidence-based framework for learning

papers.title: Strengthening the student toolbox: Study strategies to boost learning
keyFindings: Practice testing and distributed practice rated as highly effective
Highlighting and rereading rated as low utility
Evidence-based study strategies identified and ranked

papers.title: Making things hard on yourself, but in a good way: Creating desirable difficulties to enhance learning
keyFindings: Desirable difficulties enhance long-term learning and retention
Interleaving, spacing, and testing are key desirable difficulties
Short-term performance decrements mask long-term benefits

papers.title: Memory and metamemory considerations in the training of human beings
keyFindings: Introduced the desirable difficulties framework
Conditions that slow learning can enhance long-term retention
Storage strength vs retrieval strength distinction clarifies training design

papers.title: Elaborative interrogation: Generating why questions to enhance learning
keyFindings: Asking why questions enhances fact learning
Elaborative interrogation improves memory more than reading
Generating explanations deepens encoding

papers.title: Dual coding theory and education
keyFindings: Dual coding (verbal + visual) enhances memory
Imagery and language systems provide complementary encoding
Educational applications widely validated

papers.title: The generation effect: A meta-analytic review
keyFindings: Self-generated information remembered better than read information
Generation effect robust across materials and conditions
Active generation enhances encoding depth

papers.title: The production effect: Delineation of a phenomenon
keyFindings: Producing words aloud enhances memory vs silent reading
Production effect robust and easily implemented
Distinctiveness of production underlies memory benefit

techniqueGroups.name: Depth of Processing & Encoding
techniqueGroups.description: How deeply you process information during encoding determines how well you remember it.
techniqueGroups.practiceTip: Relate CSS properties to real projects you've built — self-referential encoding is the strongest form.

papers.title: Depth of processing and the retention of words in episodic memory
keyFindings: Deeper encoding produces better retention
Congruent semantic processing most effective
Encoding specificity principle demonstrated

papers.title: Levels of processing: A framework for memory research
keyFindings: Deeper processing leads to more durable memory traces
Semantic processing superior to structural or phonemic
Foundational framework for understanding encoding quality

papers.title: Self-referencing and memory: How the self drives encoding and retrieval
keyFindings: Self-referential encoding produces superior memory
Relating material to self enhances depth of processing
Self-reference effect robust across study designs

papers.title: Drawing enhances cross-modal memory plasticity in the human brain
keyFindings: Drawing enhances memory through cross-modal brain plasticity
Drawing engages motor, visual, and semantic processing
Superior to writing for memory retention

papers.title: Keyword mnemonic and how to use it effectively
keyFindings: Keyword mnemonic highly effective for vocabulary learning
Interactive imagery enhances keyword effectiveness
Systematic application guidelines established

papers.title: Emotion enhances remembering of neutral events past
keyFindings: Emotional context enhances memory for neutral information
Retroactive enhancement of memory by emotion
Emotional tagging can improve retention of mundane material

papers.title: Emotional arousal and memory binding
keyFindings: Emotional arousal enhances memory binding for central details
Amygdala-hippocampal interaction mediates emotional memory
Emotion can be leveraged for better encoding

techniqueGroups.name: Interleaving
techniqueGroups.description: Mixing different topics during practice improves discrimination and long-term retention.
techniqueGroups.practiceTip: Practice across categories, not just one at a time — the Visual Matcher mode naturally interleaves topics.

papers.title: The interleaving effect: Mixing it up boosts learning
keyFindings: Interleaving different topics enhances learning
Mixed practice superior to blocked practice for retention
Interleaving improves discrimination and transfer

papers.title: Interleaved practice improves mathematics learning
keyFindings: Interleaved math practice improves test scores
Benefits persist over extended retention intervals
Applicable to self-directed study schedules

techniqueGroups.name: Method of Loci (Memory Palace)
techniqueGroups.description: Associating information with spatial locations in a familiar environment creates powerful memory anchors.
techniqueGroups.practiceTip: Create a mental walk through your home — place each CSS property in a specific room for vivid recall.

papers.title: The method of loci in the context of psychological research: A systematic review and meta-analysis
keyFindings: Method of loci produces large effect sizes for memory
Effective across age groups and content types
Most studied and validated mnemonic strategy

papers.title: Durable memories and efficient neural coding through mnemonic training using the method of loci
keyFindings: Method of loci training produces durable memory improvements
Training reshapes functional brain connectivity
Memory athletes and trained novices show similar brain patterns

papers.title: Mnemonic training reshapes brain networks to support superior memory
keyFindings: Mnemonic training reorganizes brain network connectivity
Changes persist months after training
Novices can achieve memory champion-level performance

papers.title: Building a memory palace in minutes: Equivalent performance using virtual versus conventional environments
keyFindings: Virtual memory palaces as effective as physical ones
Rapid construction possible with digital tools
Accessibility of method of loci increased through VR

papers.title: Location, Location, Location! Demonstrating the mnemonic benefit of the Method of Loci
keyFindings: Method of loci produces significant memory benefits in classroom settings
Students effectively learn and apply the technique
Benefits persist beyond initial training session

papers.title: The method of loci as a mnemonic device to facilitate learning in endocrinology
keyFindings: Method of loci effective for complex scientific content
Students showed significant retention improvements
Applicable to self-directed medical education

papers.title: A feasibility study on the use of the Method of Loci for improving episodic memory performance
keyFindings: Method of loci feasible for episodic memory improvement
Participants successfully learned and applied technique
Self-directed practice produced measurable gains

papers.title: Optimized VR-based Method of Loci memorization techniques through increased immersion
keyFindings: VR immersion enhances method of loci effectiveness
Higher immersion produces better memory outcomes
Technology can augment traditional mnemonic strategies

papers.title: The Mind of a Mnemonist
keyFindings: Documented extraordinary synesthetic memory in patient S.V. Shereshevsky
Demonstrated that vivid multi-sensory imagery underlies exceptional memory
Foundational case for understanding mnemonic imagery techniques

techniqueGroups.name: Sleep & Memory Consolidation
techniqueGroups.description: Sleep actively consolidates memories through neural replay — it's when learning becomes permanent.
techniqueGroups.practiceTip: Study before sleep for best consolidation. Even a 20-minute nap after study boosts retention.

papers.title: The memory function of sleep
keyFindings: Active systems consolidation during sleep transforms memories
Sleep spindles and slow oscillations coordinate memory transfer
Sleep selectively strengthens relevant memories

papers.title: About sleep's role in memory
keyFindings: Sleep actively consolidates memories through replay mechanisms
Both NREM and REM sleep contribute to different memory types
Sleep-dependent consolidation enhances both declarative and procedural memory

papers.title: Sleep, learning, and memory at a glance
keyFindings: Sleep is essential for memory encoding, consolidation, and retrieval
Sleep deprivation impairs hippocampal function
Molecular mechanisms of sleep-dependent memory identified

papers.title: Sleep deprivation and memory: Meta-analytic reviews
keyFindings: Sleep deprivation before learning impairs encoding
Sleep deprivation after learning impairs consolidation
Both effects are robust across study types

papers.title: Sleep and memory consolidation in healthy, neurotypical children, and adults
keyFindings: Sleep consolidation benefits confirmed across age groups
Slow-wave sleep particularly important for declarative memory
Consistent benefits for both children and adults

papers.title: Promoting memory consolidation during sleep: A meta-analysis of targeted memory reactivation
keyFindings: Targeted memory reactivation during sleep enhances consolidation
Auditory and olfactory cues effective during NREM sleep
Effect sizes small to moderate but consistent

papers.title: The effect of sleep on novel word learning in healthy adults
keyFindings: Sleep enhances novel word learning in healthy adults
Consolidation during sleep integrates new words into lexicon
Benefits evident for both explicit and implicit word knowledge

papers.title: Sleep quality and episodic memory performance
keyFindings: Better sleep quality associated with better episodic memory
Relationship consistent across age groups
Sleep efficiency and continuity are key predictors

papers.title: Sleep spindles and memory consolidation
keyFindings: Sleep spindles positively associated with memory consolidation
Sigma-band activity predicts learning gains
Spindle-slow oscillation coupling enhances memory transfer

papers.title: A systematic and meta-analytic review of the impact of sleep restriction on memory formation
keyFindings: Sleep restriction impairs memory formation across domains
Effects are dose-dependent on restriction severity
Declarative memory is particularly vulnerable

papers.title: Daytime naps, motor memory consolidation and regionally specific sleep spindles
keyFindings: Daytime naps consolidate motor memory
Nap spindle activity predicts memory improvement
Brief naps sufficient for consolidation benefit

techniqueGroups.name: Exercise & Memory
techniqueGroups.description: Physical exercise improves memory through increased BDNF, hippocampal neurogenesis, and enhanced blood flow.
techniqueGroups.practiceTip: A brisk 20-minute walk before study enhances encoding. Regular exercise grows your hippocampus.

papers.title: Exercise training increases size of hippocampus and improves memory
keyFindings: Aerobic exercise increases hippocampal volume by 2%
Hippocampal growth correlated with memory improvement
Exercise reverses age-related hippocampal volume loss

papers.title: Effectiveness of exercise for improving cognition, memory and executive function
keyFindings: Exercise improves cognition, memory, and executive function
Aerobic exercise shows strongest effects
Benefits observed across age groups in healthy adults

papers.title: The effects of cardiovascular exercise on human memory: A review with meta-analysis
keyFindings: Cardiovascular exercise improves long-term memory
Acute exercise enhances memory consolidation
Exercise timing relative to learning matters

papers.title: Acute and chronic exercise effects on human memory
keyFindings: Both acute and chronic exercise improve memory
Acute exercise enhances memory encoding and consolidation
Chronic exercise produces structural brain changes supporting memory

papers.title: Aerobic exercise improves episodic memory in late adulthood
keyFindings: Aerobic exercise improves episodic memory in older adults
Effects moderate and clinically meaningful
Exercise intensity and duration modulate benefit

papers.title: Exercise and hippocampal volume: A meta-analysis
keyFindings: Exercise associated with larger hippocampal volume
Aerobic exercise shows strongest hippocampal effects
Volume changes linked to memory performance

papers.title: Aerobic exercise and neurocognitive performance
keyFindings: Aerobic exercise improves neurocognitive performance
Attention and processing speed show largest improvements
Memory benefits moderate but significant

papers.title: A systematic review and Bayesian meta-analysis: Acute physical activity and cognition in young adults
keyFindings: Acute physical activity improves cognition in young adults
Effects strongest for executive function and memory
Moderate intensity most effective

papers.title: A single bout of exercise improves motor memory
keyFindings: Single exercise bout enhances motor memory consolidation
High-intensity exercise most effective
Exercise after practice improves retention

papers.title: Optimal exercise interventions for enhancing cognitive function in older adults
keyFindings: Mind-body and aerobic exercise most effective for cognition
Optimal frequency and duration identified
Combined interventions may provide additive benefits

techniqueGroups.name: Working Memory Training
techniqueGroups.description: Working memory capacity can be expanded through targeted training, with transfer to fluid intelligence.
techniqueGroups.practiceTip: The Timed Drill mode exercises working memory under pressure — the cognitive load is the training.

papers.title: Working Memory
keyFindings: Proposed the multi-component model of working memory
Identified phonological loop, visuospatial sketchpad, and central executive
Foundational model underpinning all subsequent working memory training research

papers.title: Improving fluid intelligence with training on working memory
keyFindings: Working memory training can improve fluid intelligence
Transfer effects dose-dependent on training duration
Dual n-back task effective for cognitive enhancement

papers.title: Working memory training revisited: A multi-level meta-analysis
keyFindings: N-back training produces reliable near-transfer to working memory
Far transfer to fluid intelligence is small
Training gains maintained at follow-up

papers.title: Do brain-training programs work?
keyFindings: Evidence for broad transfer from brain training is weak
Some programs show near-transfer benefits
Critical evaluation needed before commercial claims

papers.title: Short- and long-term benefits of cognitive training
keyFindings: Cognitive training can be effective and durable
Effects transfer to untrained tasks in some conditions
Working memory and executive function training most promising

papers.title: Dual n-back working memory training evinces superior transfer effects compared to the method of loci
keyFindings: N-back training shows broader transfer than method of loci
Method of loci superior for direct memorization tasks
Different training approaches have complementary benefits

papers.title: Near and far transfer effects of working memory training
keyFindings: Working memory training produces near transfer effects
Far transfer evidence mixed but present in some domains
Preregistered design increases confidence in findings

papers.title: Exploring the n-back task: Insights, applications, and future directions
keyFindings: N-back task is a valid measure and training tool for working memory
Multiple variants available for different training goals
Future directions include adaptive and gamified versions

papers.title: Dual n-back training improves functional connectivity
keyFindings: N-back training improves resting-state brain connectivity
Right inferior frontal gyrus connectivity enhanced
Neural changes persist after training cessation

papers.title: Transfer after dual n-back training depends on striatal activation change
keyFindings: Training transfer depends on striatal activation changes
Individual differences in neural response predict transfer
Neuroimaging can guide personalized training approaches

techniqueGroups.name: Mindfulness & Cognition
techniqueGroups.description: Mindfulness meditation improves attention, working memory, and executive function through focused awareness practice.
techniqueGroups.practiceTip: A 5-minute breathing exercise before study can improve focus and encoding quality.

papers.title: Mindfulness enhances cognitive functioning: A meta-analysis of 111 RCTs
keyFindings: Mindfulness improves cognitive functioning across 111 RCTs
Effects on attention, working memory, and executive function
Benefits observed in healthy adult populations

papers.title: The effects of mindfulness meditation on attention, executive control and working memory
keyFindings: Mindfulness meditation improves attention and working memory in healthy adults
Executive control benefits moderate but significant
Effects scale with practice duration

papers.title: Mindfulness induction and cognition: A systematic review and meta-analysis
keyFindings: Even brief mindfulness inductions improve cognition
Attention and executive function most responsive
Accessible intervention for cognitive enhancement

papers.title: The effects of mindfulness on working memory
keyFindings: Mindfulness produces small to moderate working memory improvements
Effects consistent across diverse populations
Both brief and extended programs effective

papers.title: Effects of mindfulness-based intervention on cognitive functions in tertiary students
keyFindings: Mindfulness improves cognitive functions in university students
Working memory and attention show largest gains
Practical for self-directed implementation

papers.title: A randomized controlled trial examining the effect of mindfulness meditation on working memory capacity in adolescents
keyFindings: Mindfulness meditation increases working memory capacity
Benefits observed after relatively brief training period
Applicable across developmental stages

papers.title: Brief mindfulness meditation improves attention in novices
keyFindings: Brief meditation improves attention in complete novices
ERP evidence confirms neural attention changes
Personality moderates meditation benefits

papers.title: Mindfulness training enhances face working memory
keyFindings: Mindfulness training enhances working memory for faces
Improved evidence accumulation rate underlies benefit
Brief training sufficient for measurable gains

techniqueGroups.name: Stress & Memory
techniqueGroups.description: Moderate acute stress enhances memory consolidation, but chronic stress impairs it — timing and intensity matter.
techniqueGroups.practiceTip: A little pressure helps — the Timed Drill uses beneficial stress. But chronic stress hurts memory, so manage it.

papers.title: Stress and memory: Opposing effects of glucocorticoids
keyFindings: Stress hormones enhance memory consolidation but impair retrieval
Timing of stress relative to learning determines effect
Moderate stress can benefit memory formation

papers.title: The stressed hippocampus, synaptic plasticity and lost memories
keyFindings: Chronic stress impairs hippocampal synaptic plasticity
Stress-induced memory impairment is reversible
Hippocampal vulnerability to stress affects declarative memory

papers.title: Cortisol effects on memory consolidation and retrieval in healthy young adults
keyFindings: Cortisol enhances consolidation but impairs retrieval in healthy adults
Timing of cortisol elevation relative to learning is critical
Practical implications for exam and study scheduling

papers.title: Managing stress to improve memory: A practical guide based on cognitive science
keyFindings: Practical stress management strategies improve memory
Relaxation techniques protect encoding and retrieval
Evidence-based stress reduction accessible for self-directed use

techniqueGroups.name: Nutrition & Brain Health
techniqueGroups.description: Omega-3 fatty acids, flavonoids, and Mediterranean diet patterns support memory and cognitive function.
techniqueGroups.practiceTip: DHA-rich foods (fish, walnuts) and berries support the brain structures you're training.

papers.title: DHA supplementation improved both memory and reaction time in healthy young adults
keyFindings: DHA supplementation improves memory in healthy young adults
Reaction time also improved
Benefits observed in non-deficient population

papers.title: A systematic review and dose-response meta-analysis of omega-3 supplementation on cognitive function
keyFindings: Omega-3 supplementation improves cognitive function dose-dependently
DHA shows strongest effects on memory domains
Optimal dosing ranges identified

papers.title: The relationship of omega-3 fatty acids with dementia and cognitive decline
keyFindings: Higher omega-3 intake associated with reduced cognitive decline
DHA particularly protective for memory
Prospective evidence supports long-term benefits

papers.title: Effects of omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids on brain functions
keyFindings: Omega-3 PUFAs support multiple brain functions
Memory and learning particularly responsive
Both dietary and supplemental sources effective

papers.title: Mediterranean diet and cognitive function: A systematic review
keyFindings: Mediterranean diet associated with better cognitive function
Memory particularly benefits from dietary pattern
Polyphenols, omega-3s, and antioxidants contribute

papers.title: Blueberry supplementation improves memory in older adults
keyFindings: Blueberry supplementation improves memory in older adults
Anthocyanins may mediate cognitive benefits
12-week intervention showed significant word list learning improvement

papers.title: Effect of a 2-year diet intervention with walnuts on cognitive decline (WAHA study)
keyFindings: Walnut supplementation may slow cognitive decline
2-year intervention showed neuroprotective trends
Omega-3 ALA from walnuts contributes to brain health

papers.title: Flavonoids, cognition, and dementia: Actions, mechanisms, and potential therapeutic utility
keyFindings: Flavonoids enhance cognitive function through multiple mechanisms
Neuroprotective and neuroplasticity-enhancing effects
Dietary flavonoids accessible intervention for memory

techniqueGroups.name: Cognitive Enhancement Protocols
techniqueGroups.description: Comprehensive protocols combining multiple approaches for systematic cognitive improvement.
techniqueGroups.practiceTip: Combine multiple evidence-based techniques: spaced practice + exercise + sleep for maximum effect.

papers.title: Protocols for cognitive enhancement. A user manual for Brain Health Services—part 5 of 6
keyFindings: Cognitive, mental, or physical training has clinically relevant effects
Strategic learning-based training significantly improves objective memory
Encouraging evidence for non-invasive brain stimulation for memory enhancement

papers.title: Cognitive reserve in ageing and Alzheimer's disease
keyFindings: Cognitive reserve protects against age-related memory decline
Education, occupation, and leisure activities build reserve
Active engagement throughout life maintains cognitive function

papers.title: The Potential Role for Cognitive Training in Sport
keyFindings: Working memory, attentional control, and pattern recognition are key cognitive functions
These functions may be particularly susceptible to training
Need for more ecologically valid cognitive training tasks

papers.title: The impact of social activities, social networks on cognitive functioning
keyFindings: Social engagement associated with better cognitive functioning
Memory and executive functions particularly benefit
Social activities may provide cognitive reserve
